header.title: GHS Label Quick Search
header.subtitle: Chemical Hazard Label Search
search.inputLabel: Enter CAS Number or Chemical Name
search.inputHint: CAS numbers, English names, and Chinese names are supported
search.batchPlaceholder:
Comma, newline, or tab separated
e.g.
64-17-5
67-56-1
7732-18-5
search.batchHint: Comma, newline, or tab separated. Max 100 entries.
search.batchOverLimitDetail: Batch search is limited to {{limit}} entries; you have {{count}}. Please remove {{excess}} entries before submitting.
search.errorEmpty: Please enter a CAS number or chemical name
search.errorSingle: Search failed. Please check your connection and try again.
search.errorBatch: Batch search failed. Please check your connection and try again.
results.title: Results
results.noGhsDataHint: Absence does not imply safety — consult the SDS.
detail.noGhsDataBanner: PubChem lists this chemical but has no GHS classification data. This does not mean it is safe — please consult the official SDS.
prepared.title: Prepare solution from this chemical
prepared.subtitle: Create a label for a dilution or working solution. Hazard data will be copied from the parent chemical.
prepared.formNote: This workflow does NOT re-classify the mixture. The label uses the parent chemical's hazard data with a concentration / solvent annotation. Operational fields (prepared by / date / expiry) are what you record here — they are not derived. Always verify against the official SDS before use.
prepared.recentHeading: Recent for this chemical — click to reuse these inputs
prepared.presetHeading: Saved presets for this chemical — click to reuse concentration / solvent
prepared.recentHint: Recent instances keep the operator for context, but the prepared date resets to today on reuse.
prepared.presetHint: Saved presets are recipe-only. Operational fields stay fresh on every reuse.
prepared.presetNameHint: Used only for reuse in the app. It does not affect hazard data or the printed classification.
prepared.operationalSubheading: User-entered identifiers for lab workflow. Not derived from GHS data.
prepared.sidebarEmptyHint: Prepare and print a solution once, then reprint it here later with fresh parent hazard data.
prepared.reprintParentUnavailable: The parent chemical could not be refreshed. Please search it again before reprinting.
prepared.reprintBuildFailed: Could not rebuild the prepared label from this saved entry.
detail.provenanceCacheTooltip: Served from local cache (max 24 hours old)
detail.provenanceCacheTooltipWithAge: Served from local cache · fetched {{age}} (may be up to 24 hours stale)
detail.customSettingsHint: Select the GHS classification that best fits your use case (e.g., lab-grade, industrial-grade)
detail.classificationHint: Click any classification to set as your primary. Settings are saved automatically.
compare.title: Compare GHS Classifications
compare.subtitle: {{count}} chemical(s) selected
label.title: GHS Label Printing
label.templateStandardTip: Best default for everyday bottle labels
label.templateFullDesc: Identity, pictograms, signal word, H/P statements, and responsible contact
label.templateFullTip: Best fit for shipped-container style primary labels
label.templateQRDesc: Identity + QR + one scan-first hazard teaser
label.templateQRTip: Use when the full detail should live behind the QR
label.printPurposeBody: Choose the real-world job first. Shipped-container labels keep the full hazard hierarchy; smaller QR or quick-ID labels are clearly marked as supplemental.
label.purposeShippingDesc: Full primary label with core GHS elements.
label.purposeShippingTip: Recommended default: use roomy stock and complete H/P statements.
label.purposeQrSupplementDesc: Small label that points users to detail.
label.purposeQrSupplementTip: Use only as a supplemental label when space is tight.
label.sizeSmallTip: Use when the label must stay compact
label.sizeMediumTip: Best default for standard bottle labels
label.sizeLargeDesc: More breathing room for hazard text
label.sizeLargeTip: Use when the hazard hierarchy needs space
label.smallSizeHint: Tight density works best with shorter names and simpler hazard sets
label.previewHint: Click "Print Labels" to open the print dialog. You can verify the layout in the print preview.
label.profileHint: Saved separately from per-print fields. Shown on labels to identify the responsible lab or supplier.
label.customFieldsHint: Optional — empty fields won't appear on labels
label.settingsPreviewIntro: Adjust the layout on the left and watch the live preview update immediately on the right.
label.settingsBody: Start with a stock preset, then fine-tune spacing and nudges without leaving the modal.
label.templateOverrideHint: Purpose presets choose the recommended template automatically; override only for a special label job.
label.advancedLayoutHint: Use these only when the core purpose and stock preset need extra tuning.
label.recentPrintsEmpty: Recent print jobs will appear here so you can reload the same label set in one click.
label.stockPresetsHint: Presets are a starting point. Any manual spacing or nudge change switches the layout into custom tuning.
label.useA4PrimaryHint: Keeps all pictograms and complete statements on one full-page primary label.
label.useFullPagePrimaryHint: Keeps all pictograms and complete statements on a complete full-page primary label.
label.outputPlanReadyBody: The selected stock can print the current output without hiding safety-critical content.
label.outputPlanPendingBody: Select at least one chemical to let the app choose a safe printable output.
label.outputPlanRecommendFullPage: This stock cannot carry the complete primary label clearly. Use {{stock}} and keep this smaller label as supplemental if needed.
label.outputPlanMissingProfile: Complete primary labels need responsible lab or supplier name, phone, and address before printing.
label.outputPlanSupplementalNotice: This output is printable as a supplemental label, not a complete primary container label.
label.outputPlanMissingHazardData: This item does not have enough GHS hazard content to produce a hazard label.
label.outputPlanInvalidBody: Even this complete stock is too dense for one label. Use a continuation workflow before printing.
label.outputBasicsHint: These choices directly affect the printed label and preview.
label.calibrationHint: These values are stored in the current print config so you can reuse stock-specific calibration.
label.customStockSizeHint: Enter the real label size only when the curated presets do not match your label roll or sheet.
label.previewFocusBody: This pane reflects the current template, stock preset, and visible content.
label.previewFocusEmptyBody: Select at least one chemical to preview the real content density.
label.previewRealFragmentHint: This preview reuses the same HTML fragment that gets written into the print document.
label.outputChecklistHint: Counts come from the same content model used by print preflight.
label.previewRiskReady: This combination looks balanced for the current content load.
label.previewRiskEmpty: Select a chemical to inspect live density and scan balance.
label.previewRiskName: The current name pair is long enough to pressure a compact layout.
label.previewRiskPictograms: This chemical carries enough pictograms that compact templates may feel crowded.
label.previewRiskPrepared: Prepared-solution metadata will compete with hazard content on compact templates.
label.previewRiskCompactHidden: Compact templates intentionally hide secondary information so the main hazards stay readable.
label.previewRiskCompactNames: Compact labels keep bilingual names to one line each, so long names may still feel tight.
label.previewRiskProfile: No lab or supplier profile is set, so the print will stay generic.
label.previewRiskQr: QR layouts work best when the left side stays short and the QR block remains dominant.
label.previewRiskQrSupplement: QR supplement labels are not complete shipped-container labels; keep the matching SDS or primary label available.
label.previewRiskQuickId: Quick-ID labels are only for bench-side identification and should not replace the primary hazard label.
label.previewRiskShippingTemplate: Shipped-container purpose should use the Full template so identity, pictograms, signal word, H/P statements, and contact information stay together.
label.previewRiskShippingStock: Shipped-container labels should use roomy stock; compact stock will force omissions or cramped text.
label.previewRiskShippingProfile: Add the lab or supplier profile before printing shipped-container style labels.
label.previewRiskShippingBlockedDensity: This content is too dense for the current complete-label stock. Use a full-page primary stock for a complete label; QR supplement is only a secondary option.
label.previewRiskShippingDensity: This chemical has many H/P statements. Review the print preview carefully, or split supporting detail to SDS/QR while keeping the primary hazards readable.
label.previewHazardPlaceholder: Signal word, pictograms, and hazards will appear here.
label.previewIconFocus: Compact labels should keep pictograms and the signal word dominant.
label.previewScanFocus: Scan-first layouts should keep only the essentials next to the QR.
label.stockPresetSmallStripNote: Compact landscape stock for tubes and narrow bottles.
label.stockPresetSmallRackNote: Portrait stock with more rows for bench-side secondary labels.
label.stockPresetMediumBottleNote: Balanced default for common bottle labels.
label.stockPresetAvery5163Note: Common Letter-sheet bottle label size for North American office printers.
label.stockPresetAvery5164Note: Roomier Letter-sheet label for dense bottle or container content.
label.stockPresetBrother62Note: Common Brother/DK-style continuous roll width for supplemental bench labels.
label.stockPresetMediumRackNote: Wider labels for trays, boxes, and shallow containers.
label.stockPresetLargePrimaryNote: Roomiest preset when the full hazard hierarchy needs to breathe.
label.stockPresetA4PrimaryNote: Single full-page primary label for dense shipped-container content and proofing.
label.stockPresetLetterPrimaryNote: Single US Letter primary label for dense content and North American printers.
favorites.title: My Favorites
favorites.emptyHint: Click ☆ in search results to add favorites
history.title: Search History
empty.title: Start with a CAS number or chemical name, then turn GHS reference data into usable labels and records
empty.subtitle: A free quick utility for labs, classrooms, and small R&D teams. Find traceable hazard references first, then print labels, export data, or save common chemicals.
empty.workflowSearchDesc: Enter a CAS number, English name, or Chinese name to find the chemical and PubChem source.
empty.workflowReviewDesc: Check pictograms, signal words, H/P codes, and source context before returning to the SDS.
empty.workflowUseDesc: Turn results into labels, prepared-solution records, or spreadsheets for the real workflow.
empty.featureBatchDesc: Search up to 100 CAS numbers at once
empty.featureExcelDesc: Export full GHS data to spreadsheet
empty.featureFavoriteDesc: Save frequently used chemicals for quick access
error.title: Application Error
error.message: Please refresh the page. Contact support if the issue persists.
export.errorXlsx: Excel export failed. Please check your connection and try again.
export.csvFallbackHint: Server export was unavailable — saved with a local fallback.
exportPreview.title: Export Preview
exportPreview.subtitle: Confirm the current table scope and columns before downloading {{format}}.
exportPreview.scopeValue: {{count}} result(s) after current sorting and filters
exportPreview.hiddenRows: {{count}} more row(s) will also be exported.
exportPreview.safetyNote: Exported data is still a quick reference. Verify final labels against the SDS, supplier label, and local regulations. Formula-like text is neutralized by the export flow to prevent spreadsheet auto-execution.
print.title: GHS Label Print
print.qrSupplementNotice: Supplemental QR label - verify against SDS / primary label before use.
print.quickIdNotice: Quick-ID label only - not a complete shipped-container label.
print.supplementalHazardNotice: Supplemental hazard label - not a complete primary container label.
print.layoutBlocked: This label content is overflowing or clipped. Choose A4 Primary or another larger complete-label stock before printing. QR supplement is not a substitute for a complete primary label.
print.preparedNote: Prepared solution — hazard data copied from the parent chemical. Classification may differ for the actual mixture; verify against the official SDS before use.
footer.disclaimer: For reference only. Refer to official SDS.
footer.maintainedAsUtility: This free utility keeps labels and hazard content ad-free; brand presence stays in product notes, footer, and feedback paths.
upstream.bannerTitle: PubChem was temporarily unavailable for {{count}} result(s)
upstream.bannerBody: Those entries may not reflect current hazard data. Please retry before relying on this result, and always verify against the official SDS.
trust.authoritativeNote: Quick reference only. The official Safety Data Sheet (SDS), supplier label, and local regulations remain the authoritative source for final hazard classification.
trust.printFooter: For reference only — confirm against the official SDS, supplier label, and local regulations before use.
productTrust.title: Search and print first, then offer optional brand and feedback paths
productTrust.body: The tool's first job is helping labs, classrooms, and small R&D teams find GHS reference information quickly. Brand presence stays restrained and never enters hazard labels, signal words, pictograms, or H/P-code content.
productTrust.resultsTitle: Next steps after value is delivered
productTrust.resultsBody: You can report data issues or request batch-label, SDS, or QR workflows; the safety content remains ad-free, traceable, and reviewable.
productTrust.sourceBody: Search starts from PubChem and external SDS entry points. Final use still requires official SDS, supplier labels, and local regulation review.
productTrust.noAdsBody: Printed labels, hazard summaries, and warning content do not carry promotional content that could interfere with safety reading.
productTrust.feedbackBody: Data corrections, common templates, QR/SDS workflows, and internal deployment needs can be collected through the feedback path.
productTrust.cta: Report data or request workflow help
label.densityHint: This controls content density, not the physical stock dimensions.
pilot.manualEntryFailed: Failed to save manual dictionary entry.
pilot.sidebarSubtitle: Review pilot health, unresolved lookups, and dictionary growth tasks. This surface is for admin-only curation.
pilot.missQueriesHint: These are the unresolved searches that are most worth backfilling into the dictionary.
pilot.pendingAliasReviewHint: PubChem synonym candidates land here first. Approve only the ones you actually want to resolve in-app.
pilot.addManualEntryHint: Use this when the static seed dictionary needs a lab-specific name override or missing chemical.
pilot.adminAccessHint: Dictionary curation and pilot telemetry are admin-only. Enter the server admin key for this session.
